prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: Frontier orbital energies (HOMO, LUMO) and Koopmans‐style HOMO–LUMO gap (electron-volts)
Level of theory: DFT, tier "standard" = B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted RIJK (PySCF backend)
Geometry provenance: single-point on the supplied pyridine.xyz (no further optimisation; notation DFT//input-xyz)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge / multiplicity: 0 / 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: PySCF (version as in chemkit backend)
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 10 cycles; no warnings reported by the engine; integrity check ‘trustworthy’ = true. No fall-backs fired. Implicit solvation was not used; continuum effects are therefore absent.

Headline numerical result
HOMO = −7.2525992 eV
LUMO = −1.112786 eV
HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.1398131 eV (this is the value reported above under “value”).

Reproducibility
Command executed (from result JSON):
  /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --tier standard /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz --out …/pyridine_frontier_dft.json
Live engine log was written during the run to the default chemkit path (frontier_orbitals_<timestamp>.out) in the working directory; the final result JSON is saved as "pyridine_frontier_dft.json".

No engine warnings were emitted, and the calculation is assessed as trustworthy.
